summary: Machine policy for the acrylic blur effect on the logon background. The local Microsoft Logon.admx file documents the same System policy key and DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon value used by the app.

setting.name: Acrylic Blur on the Logon Background
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can blur the logon background with an acrylic effect. This policy decides whether that blur is turned off so the background stays clear.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable the acrylic blur to make the sign-in screen look clearer, simpler, or more traditional.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling acrylic blur makes the background image clearer, but it removes part of the default modern sign-in visual style.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon through ADMX_Logon and local policy files map it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon. The public documentation clearly describes the enabled policy behavior and the not-configured or disabled baseline behavior, but it does not publish a raw DWORD table, so this record models the documented enforced state plus the default unset state.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses the normal acrylic blur effect on the logon background image.

allowed_values.label: Show a clear logon background
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and the logon background image is shown without acrylic blur.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon = 1, which matches the documented enabled policy state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 RS5 and later systems exposing ADMX_Logon

states.rationale: Microsoft documents acrylic blur as the baseline behavior when the policy is disabled or not configured.

windows_defaults.notes: The default behavior keeps the acrylic blur effect on the logon background image.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who like the default modern sign-in look
avoid_for: Users who prefer a clearer non-blurred sign-in background

states.rationale: Leave the logon background blur under normal Windows control.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the standard acrylic-blurred logon background.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a clear logon background image instead.
tradeoffs: The default Windows sign-in visual style is preserved.

recommended_profiles.label: Clear logon background
intended_for: Users who prefer a less stylized sign-in screen
avoid_for: Users who prefer the default acrylic blur effect

states.rationale: Show the logon background image without acrylic blur.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the sign-in screen background to appear clear instead of blurred.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You like the default acrylic-blurred look.
tradeoffs: The logon background becomes visually clearer.
The default blur-based sign-in style is removed.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_Logon Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents logon-related policies on this page, including DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon and the baseline behavior when the policy is disabled or not configured.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Logon.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Logon.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy shows the logon background without blur, while disabled or not configured keeps the acrylic blur effect.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft Logon.admx file explicitly defines DisableAcrylicBackgroundOnLogon under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System, and the paired ADML help text documents that enabling the policy shows the logon background without blur. The app writes that exact enabled state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same machine policy surface used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the policy shows the logon background image without blur, while disabling or not configuring it keeps the acrylic blur effect.
